title: OpenAI launches Patch the Planet for open-source security fixes
friendlyTitle: OpenAI wants to help important open-source projects fix security bugs
summary: OpenAI announced Patch the Planet, a Daybreak initiative with Trail of Bits, HackerOne, and Calif to help open-source maintainers validate, patch, test, and disclose security vulnerabilities. Initial participants include cURL, NATS Server, pyca/cryptography, Sigstore, aiohttp, Go, freenginx, Python, and python.org.
friendlySummary: OpenAI started Patch the Planet with security partners. The program helps maintainers find, check, fix, and disclose real software security problems.
whyItMatters: AI can find more vulnerabilities, but maintainers need help turning findings into reviewed fixes. The useful part here is the full loop: expert validation, patch development, tests, and disclosure support instead of just more noisy reports.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because many apps rely on open-source code. Finding bugs is not enough; someone has to help fix them properly.
